THE MOTION IS ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON ALL CAUSES OF ACTION EXCEPT FOR NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFFS CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO EXPOSURE TO DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT ALSO ADDS AND RULES: THAT PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS STRICKEN IN VIEW OF TIMING OF ALLEGED EVENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Matthew S. DaVega, Brayton Purcell LLP, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiffs, and opposing party; Laura Rawson, Esq. Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, (415) 262-8576, appearing for Defendant Plant Insulation Co., and moving party. Plant's request that opposition to be disregarded as untimely, is overruled; Plant's request that the Ay Declaration be disregarded by the Court, is overruled. The Motion is argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant fails to shift the burden on all causes of action except for negligence. Plaintiffs create a triable issue of fact as to exposure to defendant's products. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are overruled. The Court also adds and rules: that Plaintiffs' Claim for punitive damages is stricken in view of timing of alleged event. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Rose M. Miller, CSR 5041
